No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education for indigenous language (4.3, 4.5), fostering cultural diversity appreciation (4.7) and relevant skills (4.4).
evidence: 培養學生族語識讀及聽說讀寫譯之綜合能力

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course prepares students for '族語認證', a skill that can lead to employment, tangentially supporting local culture and jobs (Target 8.9).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course directly promotes the social and cultural inclusion and empowerment of the SaySiyat indigenous group by teaching their language (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course explicitly teaches and safeguards intangible cultural heritage, such as origin legends and marriage customs, directly supporting Target 11.4.
evidence: 賽夏族傳統締結婚姻禮俗

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to climate change or action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: The course teaches indigenous knowledge about land ('土地命名的知識', '西雅圖酋長的土地叮囑'), relating to integrating ecosystem values (Target 15.9).
evidence: 西雅圖酋長的土地叮囑

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Promoting an indigenous language supports inclusive societies (Target 16.7) and non-discriminatory policies (Target 16.b) that recognize indigenous identity and rights.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to global partnerships or cooperation.
